entries.statement: eggs.nutrition: each 100 mg/day of dietary cholesterol raises serum total cholesterol by about 2.2 mg/dL (meta-analyses of controlled feeding trials)

canonical.statement: eggs.nutrition: each 100 mg/day of dietary cholesterol raises serum total cholesterol by about 2.2 mg/dL (meta-analyses of controlled feeding trials)

entries.statement: eggs.nutrition: the dietary-cholesterol serum response is nonlinear with diminishing returns, little further change above about 400 to 500 mg/day baseline

canonical.statement: eggs.nutrition: the dietary-cholesterol serum response is nonlinear with diminishing returns, little further change above about 400 to 500 mg/day baseline

entries.statement: eggs.nutrition: the LDL-C rise from dietary cholesterol is partly offset by an HDL-C rise, so the LDL:HDL ratio changes negligibly (about +0.02 units per 100 mg/day)

canonical.statement: eggs.nutrition: the LDL-C rise from dietary cholesterol is partly offset by an HDL-C rise, so the LDL:HDL ratio changes negligibly (about +0.02 units per 100 mg/day)

entries.statement: eggs.nutrition: in RCTs, whole eggs in metabolic-syndrome or type-2-diabetes populations do not raise LDL-C or total cholesterol and may raise HDL-C

canonical.statement: eggs.nutrition: in RCTs, whole eggs in metabolic-syndrome or type-2-diabetes populations do not raise LDL-C or total cholesterol and may raise HDL-C

entries.statement: eggs.nutrition: one 50 g whole egg contains about 72 kcal, 6.3 g high-quality protein, 186 mg cholesterol, and choline plus B12, riboflavin, selenium, and lutein

canonical.statement: eggs.nutrition: one 50 g whole egg contains about 72 kcal, 6.3 g high-quality protein, 186 mg cholesterol, and choline plus B12, riboflavin, selenium, and lutein

entries.statement: eggs.nutrition: the serum-cholesterol response to dietary cholesterol is continuous and unimodal, so hyper- and hypo-responder are cutpoints on a spectrum, not distinct biological subgroups

canonical.statement: eggs.nutrition: the serum-cholesterol response to dietary cholesterol is continuous and unimodal, so hyper- and hypo-responder are cutpoints on a spectrum, not distinct biological subgroups

entries.statement: eggs.nutrition: the between-person SD of cholesterol responsiveness is about 0.29 mmol/L, roughly half the mean, and within-person variation is as large, so one dietary challenge cannot classify an individual

canonical.statement: eggs.nutrition: the between-person SD of cholesterol responsiveness is about 0.29 mmol/L, roughly half the mean, and within-person variation is as large, so one dietary challenge cannot classify an individual

entries.statement: eggs.nutrition: the LDL-C response to dietary cholesterol is moderately heritable (h2 about 0.59), with identical-twin correlation r about 0.70

canonical.statement: eggs.nutrition: the LDL-C response to dietary cholesterol is moderately heritable (h2 about 0.59), with identical-twin correlation r about 0.70

entries.statement: eggs.nutrition: whether an individual's cholesterol response to eggs predicts their own CVD risk is unknown, an inference from population variance, never directly tested; the population mean does not transfer because the transfer bottoms out in absorption kinetics, synthesis feedback, and responder variance, not in a single effect size

canonical.statement: eggs.nutrition: whether an individual's cholesterol response to eggs predicts their own CVD risk is unknown, an inference from population variance, never directly tested; the population mean does not transfer because the transfer bottoms out in absorption kinetics, synthesis feedback, and responder variance, not in a single effect size

entries.statement: eggs.nutrition (choline): whole eggs are the densest common source of bioavailable phosphatidylcholine (one large egg ~147-169 mg), yet only ~10.8% of the US population meets the Adequate Intake (550 mg/day men, 425 women), so the AI is hard to reach without eggs

canonical.statement: eggs.nutrition (choline): whole eggs are the densest common source of bioavailable phosphatidylcholine (one large egg ~147-169 mg), yet only ~10.8% of the US population meets the Adequate Intake (550 mg/day men, 425 women), so the AI is hard to reach without eggs

entries.statement: eggs.nutrition (choline): choline is an obligatory component of the VLDL envelope, so it is required to export hepatic triglyceride; a defined choline-deficient diet (<50 mg/day) drives 77% of men and 80% of postmenopausal women into NAFLD or muscle damage within 3-5 weeks, reversibly

canonical.statement: eggs.nutrition (choline): choline is an obligatory component of the VLDL envelope, so it is required to export hepatic triglyceride; a defined choline-deficient diet (<50 mg/day) drives 77% of men and 80% of postmenopausal women into NAFLD or muscle damage within 3-5 weeks, reversibly

entries.statement: eggs.nutrition (choline): maternal choline supplementation in late pregnancy improves offspring sustained attention and processing speed into mid-childhood (Caudill 2018, 930 vs 480 mg/day; Bahnfleth 2022 7-year follow-up, p=0.02)

canonical.statement: eggs.nutrition (choline): maternal choline supplementation in late pregnancy improves offspring sustained attention and processing speed into mid-childhood (Caudill 2018, 930 vs 480 mg/day; Bahnfleth 2022 7-year follow-up, p=0.02)

entries.statement: eggs.nutrition (protein): the whole egg's Digestible Indispensable Amino Acid Score (true ileal, growing-pig model) exceeds 100 (~1.13) across boiled, fried, and scrambled, with no limiting amino acid above age 3, and additively rescues grain meals (toast DIAAS 25-31) to >75

canonical.statement: eggs.nutrition (protein): the whole egg's Digestible Indispensable Amino Acid Score (true ileal, growing-pig model) exceeds 100 (~1.13) across boiled, fried, and scrambled, with no limiting amino acid above age 3, and additively rescues grain meals (toast DIAAS 25-31) to >75

entries.statement: eggs.nutrition (protein): a 50 g egg provides ~543 mg leucine (~8.6% of its protein), above the mTORC1 threshold that drives muscle protein synthesis, unhindered by plant anti-nutrients, clinically salient against age-related anabolic resistance

canonical.statement: eggs.nutrition (protein): a 50 g egg provides ~543 mg leucine (~8.6% of its protein), above the mTORC1 threshold that drives muscle protein synthesis, unhindered by plant anti-nutrients, clinically salient against age-related anabolic resistance

entries.statement: eggs.nutrition (satiety): an egg breakfast suppresses the orexigenic hormone ghrelin (lower AUC) and raises the anorexigenic hormone PYY (e.g. p=0.0001 in adolescents), a direct measured endocrine shift

canonical.statement: eggs.nutrition (satiety): an egg breakfast suppresses the orexigenic hormone ghrelin (lower AUC) and raises the anorexigenic hormone PYY (e.g. p=0.0001 in adolescents), a direct measured endocrine shift

entries.statement: eggs.nutrition (satiety): the behavioral layer is noisier than the hormonal one: an egg breakfast cuts next-meal intake ~164 kcal in overweight adults (Vander Wal 2005), but in adolescents the PYY surge fails to change intake at all (Liu 2015), so the endocrine signal does not reliably transfer to eating behavior

canonical.statement: eggs.nutrition (satiety): the behavioral layer is noisier than the hormonal one: an egg breakfast cuts next-meal intake ~164 kcal in overweight adults (Vander Wal 2005), but in adolescents the PYY surge fails to change intake at all (Liu 2015), so the endocrine signal does not reliably transfer to eating behavior

entries.statement: eggs.nutrition (glycemic): the egg carries ~0.36 g carbohydrate, so its glycemic index and load are effectively zero; substituting egg protein and unsaturated fat for refined starch lowers HOMA-IR (p=0.028) in overweight adults

canonical.statement: eggs.nutrition (glycemic): the egg carries ~0.36 g carbohydrate, so its glycemic index and load are effectively zero; substituting egg protein and unsaturated fat for refined starch lowers HOMA-IR (p=0.028) in overweight adults

entries.statement: eggs.nutrition (glycemic): a high-egg diet (>=12/week) does not worsen long-term glycemic control versus a low-egg diet (<2/week) in prediabetes/T2D over 3-12 months (DIABEGG: no HbA1c, fasting glucose, or inflammation difference)

canonical.statement: eggs.nutrition (glycemic): a high-egg diet (>=12/week) does not worsen long-term glycemic control versus a low-egg diet (<2/week) in prediabetes/T2D over 3-12 months (DIABEGG: no HbA1c, fasting glucose, or inflammation difference)

entries.statement: eggs.nutrition (lipid floor): fractional cholesterol absorption is highly individual (20-80%, mean ~55%), governed by the opposing NPC1L1 uptake transporter (the ezetimibe target) and the ABCG5/ABCG8 efflux pump, independent of the absolute dietary load

canonical.statement: eggs.nutrition (lipid floor): fractional cholesterol absorption is highly individual (20-80%, mean ~55%), governed by the opposing NPC1L1 uptake transporter (the ezetimibe target) and the ABCG5/ABCG8 efflux pump, independent of the absolute dietary load

entries.statement: eggs.nutrition (lipid floor): increased dietary cholesterol compensatorily downregulates endogenous synthesis via SCAP-SREBP2 (HMG-CoA reductase suppression); endogenous synthesis (~70-80%, ~850 mg/day) buffers dietary intake (26-650 mg) in a graded fashion

canonical.statement: eggs.nutrition (lipid floor): increased dietary cholesterol compensatorily downregulates endogenous synthesis via SCAP-SREBP2 (HMG-CoA reductase suppression); endogenous synthesis (~70-80%, ~850 mg/day) buffers dietary intake (26-650 mg) in a graded fashion

entries.statement: eggs.nutrition (lipid floor): the population splits into ~66-75% hypo-responders (<0.05 mmol/L rise per 100 mg) and ~25-33% hyper-responders (>0.06 mmol/L), set by baseline synthesis, absorption efficiency, and APOE E4 (~13.7-20%); both keep the LDL:HDL ratio, so the variance is in magnitude, not ratio

canonical.statement: eggs.nutrition (lipid floor): the population splits into ~66-75% hypo-responders (<0.05 mmol/L rise per 100 mg) and ~25-33% hyper-responders (>0.06 mmol/L), set by baseline synthesis, absorption efficiency, and APOE E4 (~13.7-20%); both keep the LDL:HDL ratio, so the variance is in magnitude, not ratio

entries.statement: eggs.nutrition (TMAO): unabsorbed choline is cleaved to TMA by gut cutC bacteria and oxidized to TMAO by hepatic FMO3; the magnitude is matrix-dictated, and four whole eggs/day did NOT raise fasting TMAO (Wilcox 2021, p=0.28) while an equivalent choline-bitartrate supplement did (p<0.0001)

canonical.statement: eggs.nutrition (TMAO): unabsorbed choline is cleaved to TMA by gut cutC bacteria and oxidized to TMAO by hepatic FMO3; the magnitude is matrix-dictated, and four whole eggs/day did NOT raise fasting TMAO (Wilcox 2021, p=0.28) while an equivalent choline-bitartrate supplement did (p<0.0001)

entries.statement: eggs.nutrition (TMAO): fasting plasma TMAO is dose-dependently associated with major adverse cardiovascular events (Tang 2013, adjusted HR 2.54, 95% CI 1.96-3.28; meta HR 1.41), a settled statistical association in sick populations

canonical.statement: eggs.nutrition (TMAO): fasting plasma TMAO is dose-dependently associated with major adverse cardiovascular events (Tang 2013, adjusted HR 2.54, 95% CI 1.96-3.28; meta HR 1.41), a settled statistical association in sick populations

entries.statement: eggs.nutrition (TMAO): that TMAO is the causal atherogenic agent driving that association (rather than a proxy) is a leap the gate holds at the association grade at most; the report proposes settled risk for the association but needs-refinement for causation, so the causal claim earns no floor

canonical.statement: eggs.nutrition (TMAO): that TMAO is the causal atherogenic agent driving that association (rather than a proxy) is a leap the gate holds at the association grade at most; the report proposes settled risk for the association but needs-refinement for causation, so the causal claim earns no floor

entries.statement: eggs.nutrition (fork): the same choline that grounds fetal neurodevelopment and hepatic fat export also feeds the TMAO pathway, so the net effect is a structural split dynamically routed by host microbiome and renal clearance, not a scalar to average; the food is constant, the host body is the router

canonical.statement: eggs.nutrition (fork): the same choline that grounds fetal neurodevelopment and hepatic fat export also feeds the TMAO pathway, so the net effect is a structural split dynamically routed by host microbiome and renal clearance, not a scalar to average; the food is constant, the host body is the router

entries.statement: eggs.nutrition (undercut): oily fish and seafood raise circulating TMAO 46-62x more than eggs (Cho 2017), yet fish is cardioprotective, so TMAO cannot be a universal intrinsic cardiovascular toxin (discovery grade: confirmed the paradox shatters the causal assumption)

canonical.statement: eggs.nutrition (undercut): oily fish and seafood raise circulating TMAO 46-62x more than eggs (Cho 2017), yet fish is cardioprotective, so TMAO cannot be a universal intrinsic cardiovascular toxin (discovery grade: confirmed the paradox shatters the causal assumption)

entries.statement: eggs.nutrition (undercut): TMAO tracks inversely with eGFR, and the TMAO-MACE association is nullified after adjusting for renal function (Sanchez-Gimenez 2022, eGFR mediates 58%), so TMAO reads as a proxy for failing kidneys (discovery grade: confirmed)

canonical.statement: eggs.nutrition (undercut): TMAO tracks inversely with eGFR, and the TMAO-MACE association is nullified after adjusting for renal function (Sanchez-Gimenez 2022, eGFR mediates 58%), so TMAO reads as a proxy for failing kidneys (discovery grade: confirmed)

entries.statement: eggs.nutrition (undercut): bidirectional Mendelian randomization finds genetically predicted TMAO does NOT cause CAD, MI, stroke, or T2DM; the reverse holds, T2DM and CKD causally raise TMAO (Jia 2019), reverse causality (discovery grade: confirmed)

canonical.statement: eggs.nutrition (undercut): bidirectional Mendelian randomization finds genetically predicted TMAO does NOT cause CAD, MI, stroke, or T2DM; the reverse holds, T2DM and CKD causally raise TMAO (Jia 2019), reverse causality (discovery grade: confirmed)

entries.statement: eggs.nutrition (adjustment): the US harm cohorts (Zhong 2019, +0.5 egg/day HR 1.06 CVD; +300 mg cholesterol HR 1.17) rest on a single baseline food-frequency questionnaire and do not adjust away the collinearity of heavy egg intake with the atherogenic Western pattern (higher BMI, smoking, processed red meat)

canonical.statement: eggs.nutrition (adjustment): the US harm cohorts (Zhong 2019, +0.5 egg/day HR 1.06 CVD; +300 mg cholesterol HR 1.17) rest on a single baseline food-frequency questionnaire and do not adjust away the collinearity of heavy egg intake with the atherogenic Western pattern (higher BMI, smoking, processed red meat)

entries.statement: eggs.nutrition (adjustment): the global/updated cohorts (Drouin-Chartier BMJ 2020 pooled RR 0.98; PURE Dehghan 2020 HR 0.96 across 50 countries) use repeated dietary measures and substitution analysis, in which eggs are not a Western-pattern proxy, yielding a null

canonical.statement: eggs.nutrition (adjustment): the global/updated cohorts (Drouin-Chartier BMJ 2020 pooled RR 0.98; PURE Dehghan 2020 HR 0.96 across 50 countries) use repeated dietary measures and substitution analysis, in which eggs are not a Western-pattern proxy, yielding a null

entries.statement: eggs.nutrition (crux): the harm-null contradiction resolves not to the egg's biology but to the confounding-adjustment choice: substituting one egg/day with processed red meat raises CVD risk 15% (HR 1.15) while substituting with fish/poultry is null, so the US harm signal tracks the dietary matrix, not the egg

canonical.statement: eggs.nutrition (crux): the harm-null contradiction resolves not to the egg's biology but to the confounding-adjustment choice: substituting one egg/day with processed red meat raises CVD risk 15% (HR 1.15) while substituting with fish/poultry is null, so the US harm signal tracks the dietary matrix, not the egg

entries.statement: eggs.nutrition: in diabetic men, >=1 egg/day doubled coronary heart disease risk (Hu 1999, HR 2.02, 95% CI 1.05-3.87) while healthy men saw no association (HR 1.08), the effect-modification both camps agree on

canonical.statement: eggs.nutrition: in diabetic men, >=1 egg/day doubled coronary heart disease risk (Hu 1999, HR 2.02, 95% CI 1.05-3.87) while healthy men saw no association (HR 1.08), the effect-modification both camps agree on

entries.statement: eggs.nutrition: at up to one egg per day, egg consumption is not associated with incident cardiovascular disease in large US cohorts (pooled HR 0.93)

canonical.statement: eggs.nutrition: at up to one egg per day, egg consumption is not associated with incident cardiovascular disease in large US cohorts (pooled HR 0.93)

entries.statement: eggs.nutrition: higher dietary cholesterol is associated with higher CVD mortality (HR 1.13 per +300 mg/day), and the egg-CVD association attenuates after adjusting for dietary cholesterol

canonical.statement: eggs.nutrition: higher dietary cholesterol is associated with higher CVD mortality (HR 1.13 per +300 mg/day), and the egg-CVD association attenuates after adjusting for dietary cholesterol

entries.statement: eggs.nutrition: in the diabetic subgroup, egg consumption carries a CVD SRR of 1.22 (1.08 to 1.39) at one egg per day (Godos 2021 dose-response meta-analysis)

canonical.statement: eggs.nutrition: in the diabetic subgroup, egg consumption carries a CVD SRR of 1.22 (1.08 to 1.39) at one egg per day (Godos 2021 dose-response meta-analysis)

entries.statement: eggs.nutrition: in diabetics, the pooled CVD HR is 1.69 (1.09 to 2.62) for the highest versus lowest egg intake (Shin 2013 meta-analysis)

canonical.statement: eggs.nutrition: in diabetics, the pooled CVD HR is 1.69 (1.09 to 2.62) for the highest versus lowest egg intake (Shin 2013 meta-analysis)

entries.statement: eggs.nutrition: in diabetic individuals the egg-CVD association is inverted toward harm, the one place the observational lines agree while the population-level estimate stays in tension

canonical.statement: eggs.nutrition: in diabetic individuals the egg-CVD association is inverted toward harm, the one place the observational lines agree while the population-level estimate stays in tension

entries.statement: eggs.nutrition: frequent egg consumption (>=1 per day) is associated with elevated incident heart failure (pooled RR 1.25, 95% CI 1.12 to 1.39), a distinct mechanistic pathway from coronary disease, with the ONTARGET/TRANSCEND cohort (31,544 patients) carrying the same direction (same-party research pass, no distinct-party replication)

canonical.statement: eggs.nutrition: frequent egg consumption (>=1 per day) is associated with elevated incident heart failure (pooled RR 1.25, 95% CI 1.12 to 1.39), a distinct mechanistic pathway from coronary disease, with the ONTARGET/TRANSCEND cohort (31,544 patients) carrying the same direction (same-party research pass, no distinct-party replication)

entries.statement: eggs.nutrition: in the DIABEGG randomized trial in type-2-diabetic adults, a high-egg diet (>=12 per week) over a 12-month weight-loss protocol did not adversely change HDL, LDL, fasting glucose, or other cardiometabolic markers versus a low-egg diet, the randomized rebuttal to the observational diabetic-harm inference (same-party research pass, no distinct-party replication)

canonical.statement: eggs.nutrition: in the DIABEGG randomized trial in type-2-diabetic adults, a high-egg diet (>=12 per week) over a 12-month weight-loss protocol did not adversely change HDL, LDL, fasting glucose, or other cardiometabolic markers versus a low-egg diet, the randomized rebuttal to the observational diabetic-harm inference (same-party research pass, no distinct-party replication)

entries.statement: eggs.nutrition: in the China Kadoorie Biobank daily egg consumption is associated with about 26 percent lower overall stroke risk, driven by reduced hemorrhagic stroke (about 0.90 to 0.95 per one-egg-per-week increment), so geography and stroke subtype refine the Western population null (same-party research pass, no distinct-party replication; the Western ischemic-stroke direction, from the Million Veteran Program, is not landed here for lack of a corpus source)

canonical.statement: eggs.nutrition: in the China Kadoorie Biobank daily egg consumption is associated with about 26 percent lower overall stroke risk, driven by reduced hemorrhagic stroke (about 0.90 to 0.95 per one-egg-per-week increment), so geography and stroke subtype refine the Western population null (same-party research pass, no distinct-party replication; the Western ischemic-stroke direction, from the Million Veteran Program, is not landed here for lack of a corpus source)

entries.statement: eggs.nutrition: the 2015-2020 US Dietary Guidelines removed the 300 mg/day dietary-cholesterol limit, while the final policy still urged eating as little dietary cholesterol as practical

canonical.statement: eggs.nutrition: the 2015-2020 US Dietary Guidelines removed the 300 mg/day dietary-cholesterol limit, while the final policy still urged eating as little dietary cholesterol as practical

entries.statement: eggs.nutrition: the AHA 2020 advisory allows up to one egg per day for healthy adults and two for older normocholesterolemic patients, with caution in dyslipidemia, diabetes, or heart-failure risk

canonical.statement: eggs.nutrition: the AHA 2020 advisory allows up to one egg per day for healthy adults and two for older normocholesterolemic patients, with caution in dyslipidemia, diabetes, or heart-failure risk

entries.statement: eggs.environment: conventional caged eggs emit less GHG per kg than free-range or organic (cage 2.92 vs free-range 3.38 vs organic 3.42 kg CO2-eq/kg), the feed-conversion advantage driving it

canonical.statement: eggs.environment: conventional caged eggs emit less GHG per kg than free-range or organic (cage 2.92 vs free-range 3.38 vs organic 3.42 kg CO2-eq/kg), the feed-conversion advantage driving it

entries.statement: eggs.environment: pasture and organic egg systems require more land per kg than caged (organic 4.9 to 16.9 vs caged 2.82 to 6.3 m2/kg), up to about 2.7 times more

canonical.statement: eggs.environment: pasture and organic egg systems require more land per kg than caged (organic 4.9 to 16.9 vs caged 2.82 to 6.3 m2/kg), up to about 2.7 times more

entries.statement: eggs.environment: cage systems restrict foraging, dustbathing, and wing-flapping relative to cage-free systems, EFSA concluding the cage should not be used

canonical.statement: eggs.environment: cage systems restrict foraging, dustbathing, and wing-flapping relative to cage-free systems, EFSA concluding the cage should not be used

entries.statement: eggs.environment: keel-bone fractures are ubiquitous across all commercial laying-hen housing (50 to 100 percent by necropsy), the housing ranking confounded by assessment method

canonical.statement: eggs.environment: keel-bone fractures are ubiquitous across all commercial laying-hen housing (50 to 100 percent by necropsy), the housing ranking confounded by assessment method

entries.statement: eggs.environment: confined systems use more antibiotics and carry higher antimicrobial resistance than organic (AMR prevalence 28 vs 18 percent), the least-contested advantage of extensive systems

canonical.statement: eggs.environment: confined systems use more antibiotics and carry higher antimicrobial resistance than organic (AMR prevalence 28 vs 18 percent), the least-contested advantage of extensive systems

entries.statement: eggs.environment: perennial pasture outperforms annual feed cropland on soil-health metrics (aggregate stability, bulk density, infiltration), well-managed grazing required

canonical.statement: eggs.environment: perennial pasture outperforms annual feed cropland on soil-health metrics (aggregate stability, bulk density, infiltration), well-managed grazing required

entries.statement: eggs.environment: diverse pasture and organic systems support higher species richness per hectare than intensive feed monoculture, high-intensity agriculture cutting insect abundance about 49 percent

canonical.statement: eggs.environment: diverse pasture and organic systems support higher species richness per hectare than intensive feed monoculture, high-intensity agriculture cutting insect abundance about 49 percent

entries.statement: eggs.environment: concentrated confined manure drives nitrate leaching, groundwater under lagoons reaching 64 to 167 mg/L against a 10 mg/L standard

canonical.statement: eggs.environment: concentrated confined manure drives nitrate leaching, groundwater under lagoons reaching 64 to 167 mg/L against a 10 mg/L standard

entries.statement: eggs.environment: the emissions-land-welfare trilemma has no dominant solution, intensive systems winning per-kg emissions and land while extensive systems win welfare and per-hectare soil and biodiversity

canonical.statement: eggs.environment: the emissions-land-welfare trilemma has no dominant solution, intensive systems winning per-kg emissions and land while extensive systems win welfare and per-hectare soil and biodiversity

entries.statement: eggs.environment: whether the total water footprint favors confined or pasture systems depends on the water metric chosen, blue-plus-gray favoring grazing and total favoring intensive

canonical.statement: eggs.environment: whether the total water footprint favors confined or pasture systems depends on the water metric chosen, blue-plus-gray favoring grazing and total favoring intensive

entries.statement: eggs.environment: a farm-scale assessment may favor pasture while a global-scale assessment favors intensification, since regenerative benefits are local and costs are often global

canonical.statement: eggs.environment: a farm-scale assessment may favor pasture while a global-scale assessment favors intensification, since regenerative benefits are local and costs are often global

entries.statement: eggs.environment: improved grazing and dairy pasture sequesters soil carbon at about 0.84 to 1.05 t C/ha/yr (IPCC Tier 1, 7,092 agricultural sites)

canonical.statement: eggs.environment: improved grazing and dairy pasture sequesters soil carbon at about 0.84 to 1.05 t C/ha/yr (IPCC Tier 1, 7,092 agricultural sites)

entries.statement: eggs.environment: poultry-litter application to corn cropland retains about 41 percent of applied carbon, adding roughly 1.88 t C/ha/yr over three years

canonical.statement: eggs.environment: poultry-litter application to corn cropland retains about 41 percent of applied carbon, adding roughly 1.88 t C/ha/yr over three years

entries.statement: eggs.environment: a regenerative laying-hen pasture sequesters soil carbon at roughly 0.8 to 1.05 t C/ha/yr, provisionally, transferred from grazing and dairy pasture with no direct laying-hen measurement

closing_condition.target: a direct multi-year soil-organic-carbon measurement under laying hens on pasture

canonical.statement: eggs.environment: a regenerative laying-hen pasture sequesters soil carbon at roughly 0.8 to 1.05 t C/ha/yr, provisionally, transferred from grazing and dairy pasture with no direct laying-hen measurement

closing_condition.target: a direct multi-year soil-organic-carbon measurement under laying hens on pasture

entries.statement: eggs.environment: integrating laying hens on pasture builds stable soil carbon at the litter-retention rate, provisionally, transferred from poultry litter measured on corn cropland, not pasture

closing_condition.target: a direct soil-carbon study of laying-hen integration on pasture rather than litter applied to corn

canonical.statement: eggs.environment: integrating laying hens on pasture builds stable soil carbon at the litter-retention rate, provisionally, transferred from poultry litter measured on corn cropland, not pasture

closing_condition.target: a direct soil-carbon study of laying-hen integration on pasture rather than litter applied to corn

entries.statement: eggs.environment: regenerative grazing sequesters 2.5 to 9 t C/ha/yr, an advocacy figure 7 to 70 times the measured meta-analytic rate, with no supporting measurement or closing condition

canonical.statement: eggs.environment: regenerative grazing sequesters 2.5 to 9 t C/ha/yr, an advocacy figure 7 to 70 times the measured meta-analytic rate, with no supporting measurement or closing condition

entries.statement: eggs.environment: under attributional LCA with economic or mass allocation and manure treated as a zero-burden residual, eggs sit above plant proteins per unit protein (tofu and peas about 1.5 to 2.0 kg CO2-eq per 100 g protein against a higher egg figure)

canonical.statement: eggs.environment: under attributional LCA with economic or mass allocation and manure treated as a zero-burden residual, eggs sit above plant proteins per unit protein (tofu and peas about 1.5 to 2.0 kg CO2-eq per 100 g protein against a higher egg figure)

entries.statement: eggs.environment: under consequential LCA with system expansion, crediting the egg system for manure-displaced synthetic fertilizer drops the net egg carbon figure below that of plant-based proteins, the same biology reranked by the accounting choice

canonical.statement: eggs.environment: under consequential LCA with system expansion, crediting the egg system for manure-displaced synthetic fertilizer drops the net egg carbon figure below that of plant-based proteins, the same biology reranked by the accounting choice

entries.statement: eggs.environment: dividing impacts by a DIAAS-adjusted functional unit rather than crude protein compresses or reverses the plant-protein advantage, because lower-digestibility plant proteins deliver less usable protein per gram, so the functional unit is itself a contested value choice

canonical.statement: eggs.environment: dividing impacts by a DIAAS-adjusted functional unit rather than crude protein compresses or reverses the plant-protein advantage, because lower-digestibility plant proteins deliver less usable protein per gram, so the functional unit is itself a contested value choice

entries.statement: eggs.environment: deforestation-linked soy provenance is the dominant GHG swing variable in the layer feed supply chain, substituting South American soybean meal with zero-deforestation grain reducing the layer-system GHG by up to 22 percent (same-party research pass, no distinct-party replication)

canonical.statement: eggs.environment: deforestation-linked soy provenance is the dominant GHG swing variable in the layer feed supply chain, substituting South American soybean meal with zero-deforestation grain reducing the layer-system GHG by up to 22 percent (same-party research pass, no distinct-party replication)

entries.statement: eggs.environment: no single study applies consequential system expansion and a DIAAS-adjusted functional unit together on the same egg system, so the cross-method egg-versus-plant ranking is not settled by one harmonized measurement

closing_condition.target: a harmonized LCA applying consequential system expansion and a DIAAS-adjusted functional unit together on one egg system

canonical.statement: eggs.environment: no single study applies consequential system expansion and a DIAAS-adjusted functional unit together on the same egg system, so the cross-method egg-versus-plant ranking is not settled by one harmonized measurement

closing_condition.target: a harmonized LCA applying consequential system expansion and a DIAAS-adjusted functional unit together on one egg system

entries.statement: eggs.economics: feed conversion is about 1.95 to 2.20 kg feed/kg egg for caged systems against 2.5 to 5.3 for organic, feed being 76 to 91 percent of cumulative energy demand

canonical.statement: eggs.economics: feed conversion is about 1.95 to 2.20 kg feed/kg egg for caged systems against 2.5 to 5.3 for organic, feed being 76 to 91 percent of cumulative energy demand

entries.statement: eggs.economics: cage non-renewable cumulative energy demand is about 10.7 to 16.9 MJ/kg egg against 20.3 to 26.4 for organic, a 50 to 100 percent premium driven by feed conversion

canonical.statement: eggs.economics: cage non-renewable cumulative energy demand is about 10.7 to 16.9 MJ/kg egg against 20.3 to 26.4 for organic, a 50 to 100 percent premium driven by feed conversion

entries.statement: eggs.economics: both systems are net energy sinks, with an edible-to-input energy return of about 0.41 to 0.51 for caged and 0.27 to 0.42 for organic

canonical.statement: eggs.economics: both systems are net energy sinks, with an edible-to-input energy return of about 0.41 to 0.51 for caged and 0.27 to 0.42 for organic

entries.statement: eggs.economics: free-range organic eggs cost about twice as much to produce as caged (about $1.32 against $0.66 per dozen)

canonical.statement: eggs.economics: free-range organic eggs cost about twice as much to produce as caged (about $1.32 against $0.66 per dozen)

entries.statement: eggs.economics: the intergenerational discount rate is a value choice, Stern's 1.4 percent against Nordhaus's 4.3 percent giving present values that differ about seventeen-fold

canonical.statement: eggs.economics: the intergenerational discount rate is a value choice, Stern's 1.4 percent against Nordhaus's 4.3 percent giving present values that differ about seventeen-fold

entries.statement: eggs.economics: which objective to maximize, energy return, economic welfare, biodiversity, or resilience, is itself contested and incommensurable, so no single netted verdict follows

canonical.statement: eggs.economics: which objective to maximize, energy return, economic welfare, biodiversity, or resilience, is itself contested and incommensurable, so no single netted verdict follows

entries.statement: eggs.economics: feed is more than 60 percent of egg production cost, so a $10 per ton rise in corn shifts production cost about 0.45 cents per dozen and a $10 per ton rise in soybean meal about 0.35 cents per dozen (same-party research pass, no distinct-party replication)

canonical.statement: eggs.economics: feed is more than 60 percent of egg production cost, so a $10 per ton rise in corn shifts production cost about 0.45 cents per dozen and a $10 per ton rise in soybean meal about 0.35 cents per dozen (same-party research pass, no distinct-party replication)

entries.statement: eggs.economics: relative to conventional cages, enriched cages carry about 13 percent higher total cost and cage-free aviary systems about 36 percent higher total cost, driven heavily by pullet and labor costs (same-party research pass, no distinct-party replication)

canonical.statement: eggs.economics: relative to conventional cages, enriched cages carry about 13 percent higher total cost and cage-free aviary systems about 36 percent higher total cost, driven heavily by pullet and labor costs (same-party research pass, no distinct-party replication)

entries.statement: eggs.economics: after California Proposition 12, the retail premium rose by about $0.25 to $0.73 per dozen with an estimated annual consumer-surplus loss of $223 to $664 million, the direct pass-through of a welfare mandate to price (same-party research pass, no distinct-party replication)

canonical.statement: eggs.economics: after California Proposition 12, the retail premium rose by about $0.25 to $0.73 per dozen with an estimated annual consumer-surplus loss of $223 to $664 million, the direct pass-through of a welfare mandate to price (same-party research pass, no distinct-party replication)

entries.statement: eggs.economics: highly pathogenic avian influenza forces immediate flock depopulation, and with a roughly six-month biological lag to rebuild layer inventory the short-run supply is inelastic, so retail prices peaked at $4.82 per dozen in January 2023 (same-party research pass, no distinct-party replication)

canonical.statement: eggs.economics: highly pathogenic avian influenza forces immediate flock depopulation, and with a roughly six-month biological lag to rebuild layer inventory the short-run supply is inelastic, so retail prices peaked at $4.82 per dozen in January 2023 (same-party research pass, no distinct-party replication)

entries.statement: eggs.economics: the own-price elasticity of demand is about -0.321 for conventional eggs and -1.065 for cage-free eggs, so conventional demand is inelastic (a price shock is largely borne by consumers) while cage-free demand is elastic (same-party research pass, no distinct-party replication)

canonical.statement: eggs.economics: the own-price elasticity of demand is about -0.321 for conventional eggs and -1.065 for cage-free eggs, so conventional demand is inelastic (a price shock is largely borne by consumers) while cage-free demand is elastic (same-party research pass, no distinct-party replication)

entries.statement: eggs.economics: under the HPAI shock eggs lost their status as the cheapest complete protein, rising from about 3.0 to 6.4 cents per gram of protein, so the highest-quality common protein (hard-boiled DIAAS about 1.13) became one of the most expensive by retail price per gram (same-party research pass, no distinct-party replication)

canonical.statement: eggs.economics: under the HPAI shock eggs lost their status as the cheapest complete protein, rising from about 3.0 to 6.4 cents per gram of protein, so the highest-quality common protein (hard-boiled DIAAS about 1.13) became one of the most expensive by retail price per gram (same-party research pass, no distinct-party replication)

entries.statement: eggs.economics: because a price shock on an inelastic staple falls hardest on low-income households, a welfare mandate that raises the floor price of complete protein trades animal welfare against food security, a distributional value choice no measurement settles

canonical.statement: eggs.economics: because a price shock on an inelastic staple falls hardest on low-income households, a welfare mandate that raises the floor price of complete protein trades animal welfare against food security, a distributional value choice no measurement settles

entries.statement: eggs.economics: the long-run supply elasticity under a permanent nationwide cage-free mandate is unmeasured, so whether the Prop-12-style premium persists or erodes as producers amortize conversion is not settled

closing_condition.target: a long-run supply-elasticity estimate under a stabilized nationwide cage-free mandate

canonical.statement: eggs.economics: the long-run supply elasticity under a permanent nationwide cage-free mandate is unmeasured, so whether the Prop-12-style premium persists or erodes as producers amortize conversion is not settled

closing_condition.target: a long-run supply-elasticity estimate under a stabilized nationwide cage-free mandate

entries.statement: eggs.economics: how SNAP and low-income households substitute away from eggs toward other complete proteins during a price shock is not disaggregated, so the welfare incidence of an egg shock on food-assistance households is unmeasured

closing_condition.target: a disaggregated study of SNAP-household protein substitution during an egg price shock

canonical.statement: eggs.economics: how SNAP and low-income households substitute away from eggs toward other complete proteins during a price shock is not disaggregated, so the welfare incidence of an egg shock on food-assistance households is unmeasured

closing_condition.target: a disaggregated study of SNAP-household protein substitution during an egg price shock

entries.statement: eggs.economics: whether repeated HPAI depopulation drives long-run capital flight from the layer sector, raising the equilibrium price beyond the short-run shock, is unmeasured

closing_condition.target: a study of long-run capital investment and exit in the layer sector across repeated HPAI cycles

canonical.statement: eggs.economics: whether repeated HPAI depopulation drives long-run capital flight from the layer sector, raising the equilibrium price beyond the short-run shock, is unmeasured

closing_condition.target: a study of long-run capital investment and exit in the layer sector across repeated HPAI cycles
